FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Prediction markets hit records on World Cup betting; Macquarie bullish on Chinese AI chips.

bullets.text: Prediction market platforms Kalshi, Polymarket, and Rothera smashed June volume records on World Cup betting, with Rothera alone handling $2B in trades.
Macquarie initiates coverage on five Chinese AI chipmakers with buy ratings, positioning itself as a lead analyst voice in the sector at what it calls an optimal entry point.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: No stories in this lane today.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Gallagher Re leadership shift in Dubai as reinsurance talent flows to MENA growth.

bullets.text: Gallagher Re taps Shreyaa Vohra (ex-Price Forbes, 14+ yrs reinsurance experience) to lead Dubai DIFC facultative team—signals continued build-out in Middle East.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Specialty finance consolidation continues: LemFi expands diaspora wealth offerings, Crédit Agricole takes full control of merchant payments JV, and Thought Machine hits $100M revenue run rate.

bullets.text: LemFi clears UK regulatory approval to acquire Wealth8, expanding its immigrant-focused fintech platform into wealth management for diaspora clients.
Crédit Agricole buys out Worldline's stake in merchant payments JV Cawl to achieve 100% control; terms undisclosed.
Thought Machine reaches $100M FY2025 revenue milestone as cloud-native core banking vendor scales independently—no capital raise, exit, or M&A in pipeline.

PE & Deals.lede: Chinese PE firms recalibrating after regulatory crackdown; more selective deal environment emerging for committed LPs.

bullets.text: Chinese PE sector stabilizing post-regulatory purge—firms adopting more disciplined underwriting as capital flight abates, creating potential opportunities for patient LPs.

Markets & Macro.lede: 5 stories spanning private credit inflows, momentum trade unwind risk, AI regulatory lag, and audit framework shifts—with one M&A showcase.

bullets.text: Institutional capital is flooding into private credit despite retail outflows, signaling a potential reshuffling among managers as LP appetite diverges.
Momentum trades face elevated unwind risk this July given record positioning and thin summer liquidity—a historical pattern worth monitoring for volatility spikes.
FCA warns regulators are losing the AI arms race in financial services—lacking both speed and enforcement powers to keep pace with deployment.
PCAOB's shift from transaction-level to firm-level audit testing risks letting systemic flaws slip through the regulatory net.

Newsletters.lede: 4 stories spanning crypto volatility, ETF regulation, advisor strategy shifts, and AI unicorn IPO pushes amid pricing pressure.

bullets.text: MicroStrategy's bitcoin holdings down 52% from $126K peak to sub-$60K, testing the durability of its 'never sell' strategy and raising questions about conviction-based holding periods.
SEC intensifying scrutiny of novel ETFs including leveraged products, signaling regulatory friction ahead for emerging product launches in the space.
Advisors reassessing broad index strategies due to drag from holding low-quality/distressed names; suggests potential opportunity for more selective indexing products.
OpenAI and Anthropic accelerating IPO timelines while enterprise customers revolt against per-token pricing—cost pressure emerging before public market debut.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Philosophical mood: multiple takes on American exceptionalism, global prosperity, and whether intellectual depth justifies its cost.

bullets.text: Noah Smith argues U.S. economic/political dominance over 250 years directly enabled broad human flourishing—the correlation isn't coincidental, it's causal.
Tyler Cowen's July 4 essay celebrates ~20 things America does well (NBA, AI leadership, diversity of thought) without ranking, emphasizing pluralism over hierarchy.
Cowen, Harris, and Wheat debate whether dense French continental philosophy (Derrida, etc.) justifies study time—skeptical on practical payoff.

all.lede: 5 stories spanning macro decline (Bitcoin down 52%), fintech M&A (LemFi/Wealth8), and institutional capital flows into private credit despite retail outflows.

bullets.text: MicroStrategy's bitcoin holdings hit a 52% drawdown from October peak—testing the durability of its hold-forever thesis as BTC tumbles below $60K.
Institutional investors are doubling down on private credit funds while retail exits, signaling potential consolidation and a flight-to-quality dynamic.
Nigeria-founded fintech LemFi wins UK regulatory approval to acquire Wealth8, expanding into diaspora wealth management—a consolidation move in immigrant-focused fintech.
SEC is intensifying scrutiny of novel ETFs including leveraged strategies, signaling regulatory headwinds for emerging product launches.
